definitions: To sell goods or services at a lower price than (a competitor).
To weaken or undermine (something or someone) surreptitiously.
To cut away the material from the underside of (something).
To hit (a ball) with a downward and backward motion, imparting backspin.
examples: Large stores often undercut small businesses.
His rivals tried to undercut his authority.
The sculptor will undercut the chin of the statue.
She learned to undercut the golf ball for a better shot.
